name: Bay Of Caves
description: Explore a rugged coastline with dramatic underwater caves and swim-throughs. The site offers excellent visibility in the Indian Ocean, often exceeding 15 meters. Expect strong currents that bring pelagic life like ragged-tooth sharks and large game fish. The rocky reef hosts colorful soft corals and sponges, while deeper sections reveal steep drop-offs. Best dived in calm conditions, this spot rewards experienced divers with thrilling drift dives. Water temperatures range from 18-24°C depending on season.

name: Oddi
description: This dive site off Durban features a rocky reef with strong currents, attracting pelagic species like ragged-tooth sharks and gamefish. The topography includes ledges and overhangs, often covered in colorful soft corals. Visibility varies but can reach 15 meters on good days. Best suited for experienced divers due to the challenging conditions. The Indian Ocean's warm waters support diverse marine life year-round.

name: Hawthorn 2
description: This wreck dive off Durban sits at 30 meters, perfect for advanced divers. The Hawthorn 2, a sunken tugboat, is encrusted with colorful soft corals and sponges. Schools of gamefish circle the structure while moray eels peek from the wreckage. Strong currents bring pelagics like ragged-tooth sharks. Visibility varies but often exceeds 15 meters. The wreck's compact size allows full exploration in one dive. Nearby reefs add to the marine life diversity. Best dived in calm conditions.

name: Pyramid Rocks

name: Tiger Cave
description: This site features a dramatic underwater cave system with swim-throughs and overhangs, home to abundant marine life. Expect strong currents that attract pelagics like ragged-tooth sharks. The cave’s entrance sits at 18 meters, with depths reaching 30 meters outside. Visibility varies but often exceeds 15 meters. The rocky reef around the cave teems with colorful corals, moray eels, and schools of tropical fish. Best suited for advanced divers due to currents and depth.

name: Lionfish Hole
description: This site is a thrilling drift dive along a rocky reef in the Indian Ocean, teeming with marine life. Schools of gamefish patrol the area, while lionfish cluster in the crevices, giving the site its name. Strong currents attract pelagics like rays and sharks. The reef structure offers swim-throughs and overhangs, perfect for exploration. Visibility is typically good, and depths range from 12 to 25 meters. A must-dive for those seeking action-packed encounters in South Africa’s vibrant waters.

name: Cave
description: Explore a dramatic underwater cave system in South Africa's Indian Ocean. The site features swim-throughs, overhangs, and dark chambers teeming with marine life. Expect strong currents that bring pelagics like ragged-tooth sharks. The rocky walls host colorful soft corals and sponges. Visibility varies but can reach 20 meters on good days. Best suited for advanced divers due to surge and depth. Nearby reefs offer shelter for turtles and tropical fish. The cave's entrance sits at 12 meters, dropping to 30 meters inside.

name: Sandy Cove
region: Africa, South Africa, Western Cape, Cape Town

name: Northern Sands
description: Northern Sands offers a vibrant dive in South Africa's warm Indian Ocean waters. Expect rolling sand dunes underwater, creating a unique seascape. The site teems with colorful reef fish, rays, and occasional turtles. Visibility is often excellent, making it ideal for photography. Gentle currents suit divers of all levels. The sandy bottom hides fascinating critters like nudibranches and shrimp. Water temperatures stay comfortable year-round. Nearby reefs add variety to the dive. This spot showcases KwaZulu-Natal's rich marine biodiversity in an accessible location.

name: Romelia
region: Africa, South Africa, Western Cape, Cape Town
description: This site off Cape Town offers a thrilling drift along rocky reefs in the Atlantic. Strong currents bring nutrient-rich waters, attracting schools of yellowtail and occasional rays. The kelp forests sway with the surge, creating a mesmerizing underwater landscape. Depth ranges from 12 to 25 meters, with boulders providing shelter for octopus and colorful nudibranchs. Visibility varies but often reaches 15 meters. Cold water diving with potential for exciting pelagic encounters. Best suited for experienced divers due to currents.

name: Klein Tafelberg Reef
region: Africa, South Africa, Western Cape, Cape Town

name: Containers B&c
description: This artificial reef off Durban features two sunken shipping containers, now encrusted with colorful corals and sponges. Schools of gamefish circle the structures while moray eels peek from crevices. Strong currents bring pelagics like kingfish and occasional ragged-tooth sharks. Visibility varies but often reaches 15 meters. The shallow depth makes it accessible for most divers. The containers create swim-throughs and interesting topography on the sandy bottom. Best dived in calm conditions.

name: Nev's Ledge
description: This site off Durban features a dramatic ledge dropping to 30 meters, teeming with gamefish like kingfish and barracuda. Strong currents attract pelagics, while the rocky outcrops shelter colorful reef species. Visibility varies but often reaches 15 meters. Best suited for advanced divers due to surge and current. The ledge itself is covered in soft corals and sponges, creating a vibrant underwater landscape.

name: The Maori

name: Strawberry Rocks
region: Africa, South Africa, Western Cape, Cape Town
description: This site off Cape Town features striking granite boulders covered in colorful strawberry anemones, giving it a vibrant underwater landscape. The Atlantic waters bring cool temperatures and occasional strong currents, rewarding divers with rich marine life—expect playful Cape fur seals, shy octopus, and schools of reef fish. Depths range from 5 to 18 meters, with rocky swim-throughs and overhangs creating exciting exploration. Visibility varies but often reaches 10 meters. Kelp forests sway in the surge, adding to the dynamic scenery. Best dived in calm conditions.

name: North Light
description: North Light offers divers a thrilling drift dive along a rocky reef teeming with marine life. Strong currents attract large pelagics like ragged-tooth sharks and gamefish. The site features dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs covered in colorful soft corals and sponges. Visibility varies but can exceed 20 meters on good days. Best suited for experienced divers due to challenging conditions.

name: Justins Caves

name: Juvenile Fish Creche
description: This vibrant dive site off South Africa's east coast teems with juvenile fish in a sheltered nursery area. The shallow reef provides protection for young tropical species, offering divers a rare chance to observe their fascinating behaviors up close. Expect colorful coral formations and occasional visits from larger predators checking the buffet. Warm Indian Ocean waters and good visibility create ideal conditions for underwater photography. The site's unique ecosystem makes it perfect for both novice and experienced divers.

name: Outer Castle
region: Africa, South Africa, Western Cape, Cape Town

name: Southern Ledges
description: Explore a dramatic underwater landscape off South Africa's coast, where rocky ledges drop into the Indian Ocean's depths. The site teems with diverse marine life, from colorful reef fish to larger pelagics. Strong currents create ideal conditions for drift diving, offering thrilling encounters. Visibility varies but often rewards with nutrient-rich waters attracting abundant species. The rugged topography provides shelter for critters and creates exciting swim-throughs. Water temperatures range from mild to warm, depending on season. This dynamic site suits experienced divers comfortable with variable conditions.
